channel.title: World News Today: International News Headlines - The Hindu | The Hindu
channel.description: World news today: Stay informed with the latest international news and live updates on breaking global events from The Hindu. Explore in-depth coverage of stories from across the globe, including the Americas, Asia, Europe, Australia & Oceania, and West Asia & Africa, all reported by our dedicated correspondents.

image.title: World News Today: International News Headlines - The Hindu | The Hindu

item.title: Pakistan Defence Minister says ‘military incursion’ by India is imminent
item.description: Islamabad reinforces military on border, Khawaja Muhammad Asif says; calls on world powers to protect Indus Waters Treaty

item.title: Gaza rescuers say Israeli strikes kill at least 40
item.description: Israeli strikes kill 40 in Gaza, breaking ceasefire and escalating conflict with Hamas, resulting in civilian casualties

item.title: China, Philippines trade barbs over disputed reef
item.description: China and the Philippines dispute ownership of Sandy Cay reef in the South China Sea, escalating tensions

item.title: Russia says claims over annexed Ukraine regions key to peace
item.description: Russia asserts control over Ukrainian regions, demands recognition in negotiations with Ukraine to resolve ongoing conflict

item.title: Japan, Vietnam sign agreements on semiconductors, clean energy
item.description: Japan’s Ishiba strengthens ties with Vietnam, focusing on semiconductor industry and clean energy cooperation amid US tariff tensions

item.title: As Canada votes, Trump pushes U.S. takeover plan
item.description: New Prime Minister Mark Carney faces off against Conservative Pierre Poilievre in a dramatic Canadian election

item.title: 17 terrorists killed by security forces in Pakistan
item.description: Pakistani security forces kill 17 terrorists near Pakistan-Afghanistan border, bringing total to 71 in three days

item.title: Massive power blackout hits Spain and Portugal
item.description: Play at Madrid Open tennis tournament was also suspended, as scoreboards went dark and overhead cameras lost power

item.title: Russia declares a ceasefire in Ukraine on May 8-10 for WWII Victory Day
item.description: The Kremlin said the 72-hour ceasefire would run from the start of May 8 to the end of May 10, and Russia called on Ukraine to join it as well

item.title: Senior Indian diplomat holds talks with Taliban's acting foreign minister in Kabul
item.description: India has not yet recognised the Taliban regime in Afghanistan and has been pitching for the formation of a truly inclusive government in Kabul besides insisting that Afghan soil must not be used for any terrorist activities

item.title: Death toll in Iran port explosion rises to at least 46 killed, with over 1,000 injured
item.description: A fire still burned at the site, some two days after the initial explosion on April 26, just as Iran began a third round of negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program

item.title: Conclave to elect new Pope to start on May 7: Vatican
item.description: The 16th-century Sistine Chapel, where Conclaves are held, was closed to tourists on Monday to allow for preparations for the vote

item.title: Daily Quiz | On Conclave to elect the new Pope
item.description: The Conclave that will convene in early May to elect the new Pope. Here is a quiz on the conclave

item.title: Watch: Trump says he thinks Zelenskyy is ready to give up Crimea
Palestinian envoy tells International Court of Justice Israel using aid blockage as ‘weapon of war’
item.description: Israel's Foreign Minister Gideon Saar called the hearings part of a “systematic persecution and delegitimisation” of his country

item.title: Man suspected of killing worshipper at French mosque has surrendered to police in Italy
item.description: Murder suspect surrendered to police in Italy without elaboration; authorities suspect crime committed in souther France was possibly an Islamophobic act

item.title: China shrugs off threat of U.S. tariffs to economy, says it has tools to protect jobs
item.description: “Chinese policymakers are on heightened standby mode,” Louise Loo, lead economist at Oxford Economics said in a a report

item.title: Putin thanks North Korea for sending its troops to fight Ukraine in Kursk region
item.description: Russia first confirmed North Korean soldiers’ involvement in the battle in the Kursk region, where Kyiv’s troops seized chunks of land in August 2024, on Saturday

item.title: Nepal to witness a new political front to challenge Oli government: Baburam Bhattarai
item.description: In an exclusive interview with The Hindu on Monday (April 28, 2025), Mr Bhattarai ruled out weakening of the democratic process in Nepal

item.title: Houthi rebels claim 68 killed in alleged U.S. airstrike that hit Yemen prison holding African migrants
item.description: The U.S. military’s Central Command did not immediately respond to requests for comment

item.title: U.S. strikes kill 8 in Yemen’s capital; Houthis say U.S. conducted over 800 strikes
item.description: The U.S. is targeting the Houthis because of the group’s attacks on shipping in the Red Sea, a crucial global trade route, and on Israel

item.title: Israel's Gaza aid blockade contested in World Court hearings
item.description: In December 2024, the U.N.'s top court was tasked to form an advisory opinion on Israel's obligations to facilitate aid to Palestinians that is delivered by states and international groups, including the United Nations.

item.title: Canadians are set to vote in election dominated by Trump’s trade war and bluster
item.description: Canadians are set to decide whether to extend the Liberal Party’s decade in power or instead hand control to the Conservatives

item.title: Shanghai flaunts future of cars with screens, drones
Poll shows Musk’s clout in Washington has come at the cost of his popularity
item.description: An AP-NORC poll finds that only about one-third of U.S. adults have a favorable opinion of Elon Musk after his brief foray into the federal government

item.title: Vatican closes Sistine Chapel to public and prepares for Papal Conclave
item.description: According to a schedule determined by church law, the conclave can only begin after the nine-day mourning period. It is expected to start between May 5 and May 10

item.title: Shooting in Myrtle Beach leaves 11 injured, one dead; police fatally shoot suspect in response
item.description: A telephone message left with Myrtle Beach police was referred to the South Carolina Law Enforcement Division, which said in an email that its investigation was active and ongoing

item.title: China calls for ‘impartial probe’ into Pahalgam
item.description: Beijing ‘welcomes’ any measures to ‘cool down’ the situation between India and Pakistan; MEA declines to comment on Chinese statements, support for Pakistan in diluting UNSC statement

item.title: Netanyahu says Israeli planes intercepted Iranian ones sent to rescue embattled Syrian leader Assad
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister claimed that arch-rival Iran wanted to save Assad after watching the Iranian-backed Hezbollah group in neighbouring Lebanon suffer heavy losses in fighting with Israel

item.title: Pahalgam attack: Indian diaspora groups counter Pakistani protest in London
item.description: Indian community in London counter Pakistani protest, chanting patriotic slogans and waving flags, amid tensions over Pahalgam terror attack

item.title: U.S. in touch with India and Pakistan; urges work toward 'responsible solution'
item.description: The State Department spokesperson also said Washington "stands with India and strongly condemns the terrorist attack in Pahalgam," reiterating comments similar to recent ones made by President Donald Trump and Vice President J.D. Vance

item.title: India-Pak tensions: U.K. Foreign Secretary Lammy speaks to Jaishankar, Pakistan Deputy PM Dar
item.description: External Affairs Minister Jaishankar discusses India’s “zero tolerance” for terrorism with British counterpart amid escalating tensions with Pakistan

item.title: Vancouver car attack: Murder charges filed against suspect in car- ramming attack that killed 11
item.description: The British Columbia Prosecution Service charged Kai-Ji Adam Lo (30), with eight counts of second degree murder and said more charges were possible

item.title: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says Ukraine not kicked out of Russia's Kursk
item.description: Kyiv had hoped it could use land in the Kursk region as a bargaining chip in future peace talks with Russia, which has seized parts of eastern and southern Ukraine since launching its offensive in February 2022.

item.title: Trump says Columbus Day will now just be Columbus Day
item.description: Democrat Joe Biden was the first president to mark Indigenous Peoples Day, issuing a proclamation in 2021 that celebrated "the invaluable contributions and resilience of Indigenous peoples" and recognise "their inherent sovereignty"

item.title: Israeli jets strike Beirut’s southern suburbs for the third time since a ceasefire began
item.description: Israeli jets have struck Beirut’s southern suburbs in the third such attack since a ceasefire began in late November

item.title: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu calls for 'dismantling' Iran's nuclear infrastructure
item.description: Western governments have long suspected Iran of pursuing a nuclear weapons capability, an ambition Tehran has consistently denied, insisting its programme is for peaceful purposes only.

item.title: Pakistan, China resolve to uphold regional peace and stability, oppose unilateralism
item.description: The understanding was reached during a telephone conversation between Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar and his Chinese counterpart Wang Yi, reported Radio Pakistan.

item.title: Gaza ceasefire talks make some progress, Qatari PM says
item.description: Mossad Director David Barnea travelled to Doha on Thursday to meet Sheikh Mohammed amid efforts to reach a new ceasefire in Gaza, Axios reported last week.

item.title: Religious hate has no place in France, says Macron after man killed in mosque
item.description: Racism and hatred based on religion can have no place in France. Freedom of worship cannot be violated, says President Emmanuel Macron

item.title: Women's toilets are not for transwomen, U.K. watchdog says
item.description: The Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC) issued interim guidance for organisations in response to five judges ruling earlier this month that a woman is legally defined based on their sex at birth

item.title: U.S. deports 3 American children, including cancer patient: rights groups
item.description: The deportations from the southern state of Louisiana come as U.S. President Donald Trump pursues a hard-line immigration policy, calling for mass expulsions of undocumented migrants

item.title: Iran President visits those injured in port explosion
item.description: The visit by President Masoud Pezeshkian came as the toll from the blast at the Shahid Rajaei port outside of Bandar Abbas in southern Iran’s Hormozgan Province rose to 40 with about 1,000 others injured.

item.title: Pakistan wants Russian, Chinese involvement in Pahalgam terror attack probe
item.description: Pakistani Defence Minister Khawaja Asif said Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif also proposed conducting an international investigation

item.title: Russia files terrorism charges against suspect in car bomb killing of army officer
item.description: Interfax, citing Russia's Investigative Committee, said the suspect, Ignat Kuzin, had pleaded guilty to killing Moskalik and had said he was recruited by Ukraine's security services.

item.title: Trump urges 'free' transit for U.S. ships through Panama, Suez canals
item.description: Panamanian President Jose Raul Mulino said on April 26, that toll fees for the Panama Canal are regulated by the Panama Canal Authority (ACP), an autonomous governing body that oversees the trade route.

item.title: Seven teachers, several policemen injured during clashes in Nepal's Kathmandu
item.description: The police used baton charges and water cannons to disperse thousands of agitating teachers who tried to break the security cordon to enter the restricted area in Nayabaneshwor area of Kathmandu, according to eyewitness

item.title: Russia launches nearly 150 drones against Ukraine as Trump doubts Putin's desire for peace
item.description: The attacks came hours after Russia claimed to have regained control over the remaining parts of the Kursk region

item.title: Palestinians struggle to feed their families as Israel has blocked food from Gaza for nearly 60 days
item.description: The around 2.3 million Palestinians in Gaza are now mainly living off canned vegetables, rice, pasta and lentils, doctors warn that the lack of variety, protein and other nutrients in children's diet will cause long-term damage to their health.

item.title: Exit deadline ends today; Pakistan citizens bid tearful goodbyes to kin, rush home through Attari
item.description: Tensions rise as Pakistani nationals rush to exit India amid escalating conflict over Pahalgam terror attack

item.title: Catholic faithful pay respects to Pope Francis as his tomb opens to public in Rome basilica
item.description: The tomb was opened on the second of nine days of official mourning for Pope Francis, after which a conclave will be held to elect the next Pope

item.title: Letter written onboard the Titanic before it sank sells for almost $400,000 at auction
item.description: Rare Titanic letter by Archibald Gracie sold for over 60,000 pounds, providing unique insight into the ill-fated voyage

item.title: China seizes disputed reef near key Philippine military outpost: report
item.description: Chinese coast guard seizes disputed reef near Philippine military outpost in South China Sea, escalating territorial tensions

item.title: Pakistan initiates 'emergency' steps to secure drug supplies after trade suspension with India
item.description: India trade halt triggered "urgent measures to secure" pharmaceutical needs in Pakistan and health authorities have initiated "emergency preparedness" measures

item.title: 11 killed as car drives into Vancouver street festival crowd; police rule out terrorism
item.description: Dozens of others were injured, some seriously, in what authorities described as a car ramming attack that shook the country ahead of federal elections.

item.title: Continuity or rupture: what direction for the next Pope?
item.description: Cardinals will meet within days for a conclave to elect a new pontiff, sparking fevered speculation about how the next Pope will guide the world’s 1.4 billion Catholics

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel condemns Pahalgam terror attack, assures ‘full support’ to India
item.description: FBI Director condemns Pahalgam terrorist attack, pledges full support to India, highlighting global threat of terrorism

item.title: Australia Government touts free healthcare hotline as general election nears
item.description: Labor government pledges to enhance Australia’s healthcare system with free medical advice line and telehealth service if elected

item.title: Palestinian president Abbas appoints aide as potential successor
item.description: Creation of the post follows years of international pressure to reform the Palestine Liberation Organization and comes as Arab and Western powers envision an expanded role for Abbas's Palestinian Authority

item.title: Hamas says open to 5-year Gaza truce, one-time hostages release
item.description: A Hamas delegation was in Cairo discussing with Egyptian mediators ways out of the 18-month war, while, on the ground, rescuers said Israeli strikes killed at least 35 people.

item.title: How did WHO nations strike a pandemic deal? | Explained
item.description: What are the highlights of the new agreement? Why was it long overdue? What challenges did countries face during negotiations? How will it impact global cooperation and equity in the next pandemic? Has a balance been struck between global equity and market-based innovation?

item.title: Pakistan’s Defence Minister seeks stronger ties with Bangladesh
item.description: The event, hosted by Bangladesh High Commissioner Muhammad Iqbal Hussain Khan, came days after Bangladesh and Pakistan held a foreign secretary-level Foreign Office Consultation (FOC) in Dhaka after a gap of nearly 15 years

item.title: Trump sceptical over Putin’s desire to end Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump says he doubts Russia’s Vladimir Putin wants to end his war in Ukraine

item.title: Canada votes in election upended by Trump threats
item.description: When Mr. Carney replaced the unpopular Mr. Trudeau on March 14, he anchored his message squarely on the Mr. Trump threat, claiming the U.S. "wants to break us, so they can own us."

item.title: 49 killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza over 24 hours as mediators scramble to restart ceasefire
item.description: The number was confirmed by Gaza's Health Ministry, along with three more people who were killed in the Shati refugee camp along the city's shoreline

item.title: China insists no trade talks with U.S., despite Trump’s claim
Pope Francis buried in Rome's Santa Maria Maggiore basilica
item.description: Francis had asked that the tomb, located near the altar of Saint Francis, be simple and unadorned, reflecting the humble spirit of his papacy

item.title: Russia says all Ukrainian troops have left the Kursk region as Zelenskyy meets Trump at Vatican
item.description: All Ukrainian troops have been forced from Russia’s Kursk region, Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said Saturday

item.title: Fire rages after major blast at Iran port kills 25, injures some 800
item.description: The port took in a shipment of “sodium perchlorate rocket fuel” in March, private security firm Ambrey says; the fuel was going to be used to replenish Iran’s missile stocks

item.title: Pope Francis funeral | Mighty, meek bid farewell; Francis is remembered as a ‘pope among the people’ in his funeral Mass
item.description: World leaders and Catholic faithful bid farewell to Pope Francis in a funeral reflecting his priorities and wishes

item.title: Trump met with Ukrainian President Zelenskyy before Pope Francis' funeral
item.description: The two leaders “met privately today and had a very productive discussion"

item.title: Pahalgam attack: Indian, Pakistani protesters face off in London; Pak diplomatic official makes threatening gestures
item.description: The Pakistan official, standing next to a large banner reading “Pakistan stands with Kashmiris”, could be seen making a threatening throat-slitting gesture towards the Indian protesters across the building.

item.title: A deleted condolence after pope’s death revealed tension between Israel and the Vatican
item.description: Hours after Israel’s Foreign Ministry posted a condolence for the passing for Pope Francis, it was deleted without explanation

item.title: Iraq farmers turn to groundwater to boost desert yield
item.description: Hadi Saheb is just one of many Iraqis who have turned to drilling wells in the desert to help sustain the country's agriculture

item.title: Pakistan offers to join 'neutral, transparent' probe in Pahalgam terror attack
item.description: Pakistan’s Prime Minister said the Armed forces were “prepared to defend the country’s sovereignty”, after India “links” the attack with Islamabad

item.title: Iran’s FM Araghchi says ‘differences’ persist, U.S. calls nuclear talks ‘positive and productive
item.description: Iran and the U.S. “identified a shared aspiration to reach agreement based on mutual respect and enduring commitments,” Omani Foreign Minister Badr al-Busaidi said

item.title: M.P. begins process to identify and deport Pakistani nationals with revoked visas
item.description: State police act on MHA directive following Pahalgam terror attack; only diplomatic, official, and long-term visa holders exempt

item.title: Pahalgam attack: Pakistan Defence Minister demands international probe, NYT reports
item.description: Pakistan’s Defence Minister Khawaja Muhammad Asif has accused India of taking steps to punish Pakistan "without any proof, without any investigation".

item.title: U.N. Security Council 'strongly condemns' Pahalgam terror attack
item.description: China, Pakistan join rest of the Security Council members in condemning the terror strike in ‘strongest terms’; Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif says his government is ready for a neutral investigation

item.title: Seating plan for a pope's funeral – it's complicated, or compliqué
item.description: Pope Francis’ funeral seating arrangements based on French alphabet, with Trump likely in front rows

item.title: Pakistan violates ceasefire along LoC in Kashmir, army responds appropriately: Defence official
item.description: Pakistan Army violated ceasefire in Kashmir, prompting Indian army response; no casualties reported, defense official confirms

item.title: Gunmen kill at least 20 people in mining town in northwestern Nigeria, rights group says
item.description: Kidnappings for ransom have become a lucrative way for bandit groups to fund other crimes and control villages in the restive region

item.title: Virginia Giuffre, who accused Britain’s Prince Andrew in Epstein sex trafficking scandal, dies
item.description: Giuffre killed herself on Friday (April 25, 2025) at her farm in Western Australia, her publicist confirmed

item.title: There has always been tension between India, Pak; they'll get it figured out: Trump
item.description: The U.S. Donald Trump was asked about tensions between India and Pakistan in the wake of the Pahalgam terror attack

item.title: Pope Francis funeral updates: Pope buried in Santa Maria Maggiore basilica
item.description: Pope’s coffin arrives for burial at Rome basilica; he is remembered as a ‘pope among the people’ in his funeral Mass

item.title: India’s decision on Indus casts shadow on renewal of India-Bangladesh Ganga Water Treaty: Bangladesh water expert
item.description: “Renewal of the Ganga treaty will come up next year but if India continues to hold the Indus Waters Treaty in abeyance then it will create doubts about India’s willingness on sharing of river waters with Bangladesh”

item.title: Rescuers say death toll from Israeli strike on north Gaza home rises to 23
item.description: Civil defence teams recovered 11 bodies last night and this morning following the Israeli bombing that targeted a residential house ... in Jabalia, says an official.

item.title: Trump signs order to ramp up U.S. deep-sea mining
item.description: Washington wants to lead efforts to scoop up mineral-rich deep-sea nodules and other material from the seabed, sidestepping an international regulatory effort and overriding the concerns of environmentalists.

item.title: Trump says ‘Crimea will stay with Russia’ as he seeks end to war in Ukraine
item.description: Crimea is a strategic peninsula along the Black Sea in southern Ukraine. It was seized by Russia in 2014, while U.S. President Barack Obama was in office, years before the full-scale invasion that began in 2022.

item.title: China considers exempting some goods from U.S. tariffs, source says
item.description: A Ministry of Commerce taskforce is collecting lists of items that could be exempted from tariffs and is asking companies to submit their own requests, according to a source who spoke on condition of anonymity.

item.title: Rubio to host signing of agreement between Rwanda, DR Congo
item.description: The State Department on its public schedule said that Mr. Rubio would sign a "declaration of principles" with DRC Foreign Minister Therese Kayikwamba Wagner and Rwandan Foreign Minister Olivier Nduhungirehe

item.title: Former South Korean President Moon calls his bribery indictment ’unjust’
item.description: Former South Korean President Moon Jae-in has called his bribery indictment “unjust” and accused prosecutors of abusing their power in what he called a politically motivated investigation

item.title: Magnitude 6.3 earthquake hits Ecuador coast, no tsunami threat
item.description: Monitors said the epicentre was near the Pacific coast of Esmeraldas, at a depth of about 35 kilometres

item.title: U.S. funding cuts likely to impact long-term research in malaria: Jane Carlton
item.description: Dr. Jane Carlton is a globally recognized leader in malaria genomics and director of the Johns Hopkins Malaria Research Institute. In an email interview, she details the impact of aid suspension and funding cuts on malaria control, discusses the newest breakthroughs in research and warns of climate change potentially increasing malaria cases worldwide says that though India will not see any immediate adverse effect of the US funding cut to the global malaria programme it will, however, impact the national research, training and teaching of the next generation of malaria researchers

item.title: Houthi rebels have shot down seven U.S. Reaper drones worth $200 million in recent weeks
item.description: Houthi rebels in Yemen have shot down seven U.S. Reaper drones in less than six weeks

item.title: Pakistan doing West’s dirty work for decades: Pakistan Defence Minister
item.description: Asked if Pakistan fears an escalation as a result of the Pahalgam terrorist attack, the Minister said the country is prepared to respond

item.title: Pakistan Senate passes resolution rejecting India's 'attempt' to link Pahalgam terror attack with Islamabad
item.description: The resolution comes a day after the Pakistan National Security Committee meeting by top civilian and military leaders in Pakistan put the Simla Agreement and other bilateral accords with India on hold

item.title: Top Russian general killed in car bombing near Moscow
item.description: Lt. Gen. Yaroslav Moskalik, a deputy head of the main operational department in the General Staff of the Russian armed forces, was killed by an explosive device placed in his car in Balashikha

item.title: China vows in politburo meeting to support firms, workers affected by U. S. tariffs
item.description: China vows to stabilise jobs by increasing the proportion of unemployment insurance funds that can be returned to companies that are greatly affected by tariffs

item.title: U.S. Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth's chief of staff Joe Kasper leaving his job
item.description: "Joe Kasper will continue to serve President Trump as a Special Government Employee handling special projects at the Department of Defence," an official said
